title: Fix admin menu item visibility in UserDropdown

requirements.text: Fix the Admin panel link in UserDropdown so it reliably appears for authenticated admin users. Audit the full chain: (1) the backend admin check endpoint/method, (2) the useIsCallerAdmin() hook to ensure it calls the backend using the authenticated identity (not anonymous), (3) the React Query cache key and stale/refetch behavior so the result updates after login, and (4) the conditional rendering logic in UserDropdown that gates the Admin link on the hook's return value. All four layers must be consistent and correct.

quotes: Admin option not showing in dropdown menu (persists after multiple fix attempts)

acceptanceCriteria: After logging in with an admin Internet Identity, the 'Admin' menu item is visible in the UserDropdown without requiring a page refresh.
Non-admin authenticated users do not see the Admin menu item.
Unauthenticated users do not see the Admin menu item.
Navigating to /admin via the dropdown link works correctly for admin users.
The useIsCallerAdmin() hook uses the authenticated actor (not the anonymous actor) when querying the backend.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts directly — work around them using safe composition patterns.
Do not change the existing role-based access control logic in backend/main.mo beyond what is needed to expose a reliable isCallerAdmin query.
nonGoals: Adding new admin features or dashboard functionality.
Changing the visual design of the UserDropdown.
Modifying authentication flow or Internet Identity integration.
